mcdlssg.algo.description.header:
FSR1 - AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution 1
FSR2 - AMD FidelityFX Super Resolution 2
NIS - NVIDIA Image Scaling
SGSR - Snapdragon™ Game Super Resolution

mcdlssg.screen.config.name: Super Resolution Configuration
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.label.disa: Disable Super Resolution when shaders are off
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.label.paus: Pause game when configuration screen is open
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.di: Automatically disable super resolution when shaders are off
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.en: Use a compatibility shader compiler when enabled
Enable GPU profiling for bottleneck analysis, may affect performance.
When enabled, certain incomplete features (such as DLSS, XeSS) will become available. These features may be unstable, cause unknown issues, or even crash the game. Use with caution.
Generate additional frames through Streamline and the built-in Vulkan presentation backend. Requires Windows, a compatible NVIDIA GPU, and a game restart.
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.dl: Total displayed frames per rendered frame. Multi-frame generation above 2x requires an RTX 50 series GPU; the value is clamped to what the driver supports.
Provide HUD-less and UI resources to DLSS Frame Generation to reduce crosshair, text, and interface ghosting.
Show the DLSS Frame Generation mode and the real/generated frame rates in the top-right corner of the screen.
Motion vector source. Auto uses shaderpack vectors or depth reprojection; Reprojection forces depth reprojection; Disabled lets DLSS G estimate motion internally (debug).
Fill cutout holes with nearby valid motion vectors to reduce foliage edge artifacts.
Zero out sub-pixel motion vector noise to reduce texture shimmering when nearly static.
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.en: Only effective in development environment, enables debug UI
When enabled, the mod loads the selected OptiScaler DLL file early during game startup.
Only effective in development environment, enables RenderDoc integration
When enabled, uses the selected algorithm to super resolve the image
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.fo: When enabled, the mod will ignore the shader pack's super resolution compatibility profile and perform scaling in normal mode
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.ge: Motion vectors can improve the super resolution quality of some algorithms (FSR2, SGSR2), but will cost performance (deprecated)
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.in:
Some mods use PostChain for UI effects.
Injecting code into PostChain by this mod will cause UI effects to render at world resolution, causing rendering issues (e.g., blurred ModernUI interface).
Add the names of PostChains you wish to exclude here, and the mod will skip them.
Changes may require a game restart to take effect.

The precision of the internal texture format affects VRAM usage: higher precision means higher usage; lower precision means lower usage.
Note: Too low precision may cause visible color banding.

The mod uses OpenGL-Vulkan interop for super resolution (only for some algorithms, like DLSS). Interop sync mode determines how the mod synchronizes resources between OpenGL and Vulkan.
Low Latency mode processes the current frame directly, synchronizing OpenGL and Vulkan, resulting in lower latency but may reduce low frame rates.
Performance mode allows super resolution on Vulkan to run concurrently with game rendering on OpenGL, improving performance but increasing input lag. This mode is unstable and offers minor gains.
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.op: The OptiScaler DLL file to load.
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.sh:
Lower values make edges more distinct
Higher values make distant objects blurrier
Recommended value: around 0.55
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.sk: Skipping Vulkan initialization may affect some algorithms.
Skipping native library loading may cause malfunctions.
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.th: Adjust the contrast of the UI color scheme (-1 = lowest, 0 = standard, 1 = highest)
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.up:
Higher values mean lower rendering resolution, blurrier image, and lower performance cost.
Lower values mean higher rendering resolution, sharper image, and higher performance cost.
Current rendering resolution: %sx%s
Current rendering precision: %s
Recommended value: 1.4~1.8
mcdlssg.screen.config.special.fsr.versio:
FSR version: 2.3.3 for FSR2, 3.1.4 for FSR3.
Default is 2.3.3
Enabling may improve performance but will slightly reduce image quality
Enabling may improve performance but will slightly reduce image quality
mcdlssg.screen.config.special.fsr2.versi:
FSR2 version, there are some differences between versions.
Default is 2.3.3
mcdlssg.screen.config.special.sgsr2.vari:
CS_2 (Compute Shader/2 Post-Process Passes): moderate performance, moderate quality
CS_3 (Compute Shader/3 Post-Process Passes): worse performance, best quality
FS_2 (Fragment Shader/2 Post-Process Passes): best performance, worse quality
mcdlssg.screen.config.warn.algorithm_inc: Warning: The selected algorithm is incomplete and cannot be used properly
mcdlssg.screen.config.warn.algorithm_uns: Warning: The selected algorithm is unstable
mcdlssg.screen.config.warn.mobile_device: Warning: Running on a mobile device. Support for mobile devices is unstable and may cause unexpected errors.
mcdlssg.screen.debug.performance_data:
Frame time %1$sms
World render time %2$sms
Upscale algorithm time %3$sms
mcdlssg.screen.info.name: Information
mcdlssg.screen.info.text.author: Author
mcdlssg.screen.info.text.open_source_tha: Thanks to the authors and contributors of the following libraries. Without their work, this mod would not exist.
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.al: This algorithm is experimental, unstable, and may cause crashes or errors

This algorithm is experimental, unstable, and may cause crashes or errors
To use it, please enable Experimental > Enable Experimental Features
This algorithm is not supported. Reasons:
, please disable Advanced > Skip Vulkan Initialization
, if you have already disabled Skip Vulkan Initialization, please try restarting the game
mcdlssg.screen.config.options.tooltip.up: Custom ratio is not supported for this algorithm
mcdlssg.screen.config.hint.performance_w: No shader pack is currently active. Vanilla Minecraft has a low graphics load, so super resolution cannot improve performance and may cause frame rate drops. Compatibility in vanilla mode is limited and may cause rendering conflicts with some mods.
mcdlssg.screen.config.hint.b3d_vulkan_un: Super Resolution cannot work on the Vulkan backend

The Vulkan graphics backend is currently in use. Because Iris does not support the Vulkan backend yet, Super Resolution cannot enable upscaling or scaling in this environment; you can still open the configuration screen, but all features will be unavailable.
mcdlssg.screen.config.hint.shader_compat: The current shader pack has not been adapted for the super resolution mod and will run in compatibility mode. This mode may cause: blurry visuals, ghosting artifacts, performance degradation, and reduced compatibility with other mods.
